title: Degrees of Freedom
subtitle: How Many Ways Can It Move?

screens.title: Wiggle Your Arm. Count Every Direction It Can Move.
screens.content:
Right now, lift your arm. How many different ways can it move?

- Rotate at the shoulder (up-down)
- Rotate at the shoulder (forward-backward)
- Rotate at the shoulder (twist)
- Bend at the elbow
- Rotate at the wrist
- Bend at the wrist (forward-backward)
- Rotate your wrist (twist)

That's 7 movements, or **7 degrees of freedom (DOF)**. Your arm is incredibly flexible.

A robot arm isn't. Most industrial robots have 6 DOF. A simple pick-and-place robot might have only 3. A door hinge? Just 1.

screens.title: What Are Degrees of Freedom?
screens.content:
A degree of freedom is one independent direction in which something can move.

**In 3D space, there are 6 possible DOF:**

1. **Translation along X-axis** (left-right)
2. **Translation along Y-axis** (up-down)
3. **Translation along Z-axis** (forward-backward)
4. **Rotation around X-axis** (pitch тАФ nose up/down)
5. **Rotation around Y-axis** (yaw тАФ turn left/right)
6. **Rotation around Z-axis** (roll тАФ tilt left/right)

A robot's **DOF = the number of independent joints.**

Each joint typically adds one DOF (or sometimes two for special joints). A robot that can move left-right, up-down, forward-backward, AND rotate at the end = 4 DOF minimum.

Why does DOF matter? A robot needs at least 6 DOF to reach any point in space AND orient itself in any direction. Less than 6, and you can reach a point but can't control your orientation. More than 6, and you have redundancy тАФ multiple ways to reach the same position.

screens.title: Joint Types and Their DOF
screens.content:
Different joints provide different DOF:

**REVOLUTE JOINT (1 DOF)**
Rotates around one axis, like a door hinge.
Example: Elbow bending (rotate around one axis)

**PRISMATIC JOINT (1 DOF)*
Slides along one axis, like a drawer.
Example: A piston moving back-and-forth

*(SPHENICAL JOINT (3 DOF)

screens.title: Explore Degrees of Freedom
screens.content: Interact with this robot arm. Each slider represents one joint. Move each slider to see how DOF controls motion. Notice how with 6 DOF, you can reach anywhere and point in any direction.

screens.title: Designing a Robot for Manufacturing
screens.content:
You're building a robot for a phone factory. The robot must:
- Pick up a phone from a conveyor belt (position in 3D space)
- Rotate it to scan a QR code on the back (orientation matters)
- Place it in a box rotated correctly

**Minimum DOF needed:** 6

**Design:**
- Base rotation (1 DOF) тАФ sweep side to side
- Shoulder (1 DOF) тАФ reach high and low
- Elbow (1 DOF) тБкІSacj far and near
- Wrist 1 (1 DOF) тАФ pitch the gripper
- Wrist 2 (1 DOF) тАФ yaw the gripper
- Gripper Rotate (1 DOF) тАФ roll the gripper

With 6 DOF, the robot can reach any phone position AND orient the gripper to scan from any angle. It's the industry standard for a reason.

screens.title: DOF Analysis
screens.content:
Analyze these machines and count their DOF:

1. A door on a hinge (can only swing open-closed) = ?
2. A robotic vacuum (can move forward-backward, turn left-right, but not lift) = ?
3. A crane that moves on a rail (can move forward-backward on the rail, lift up-down, and swing left-right) = ?
4. Your laptop screen (can only tilt up-down) = ?

Use the DOF explorer to verify your answers.

scenarios.name: Door Hinge
scenarios.hint: A door only swings around one axis. That's 1 revolute joint.

scenarios.name: Robot Vacuum
scenarios.hint: It can move forward-backward (1), turn left-right (1), and spin its brushes (1). That's 3 DOF.

scenarios.name: Crane on Rail
scenarios.hint: Forward-backward on rail (1), lift up-down (1), swing side-to-side (1). That's 3 DOF.

scenarios.name: Laptop Screen
scenarios.hint: Tilts up-down around one hinge. That's 1 DOF.

question.prompt: How many degrees of freedom does a standard door hinge have?

options.feedback: Correct! A door hinge is a revolute joint that rotates around a single axis. It can only open and close, so it has 1 DOF.
A standard hinge only allows rotation around one axis. It's not a universal joint.
A door hinge is fixed in place. It can only rotate around its pivot axis, giving 1 DOF.

options.text: 6 DOF (full freedom like a robot arm)
options.feedback: A door hinge is the opposite of a 6-DOF robot. It's completely restricted to one axis of motion.
